concerns.text: Constitution Principle VII states 'All statistical claims MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' while FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. The Assumptions section documents an exception but the Constitution itself was NOT amended. This is a direct constitutional contradiction that must be resolved by either amending Principle VII to add the census-data exception clause, or revising FR-006 to align with the Constitution.

FR-003 contains excessive inline caveats ('Phase 1 Implementation Exclusion', 'Phase 2+ Timeline', 'Threshold Distinction', etc.) creating maintenance burden. Per maintainer decision, move Phase 2+ scope notes to dedicated section or Assumptions. Keep one concise Phase-1 note only.

SC-005 and SC-010 are Phase 2+ scope items anchored to User Story 2. Consider adding explicit '(Phase 2+)' prefix to make scope boundary clearer in Success Criteria section, matching the pattern used in FR-003.
